title: Rent vs Buy: How to Decide and Save ₹2 Lakh in 5 Years

twitter_thread.tweet1: Is buying a home always an investment? Most Indians lose ₹2 Lakh in just 5 years to hidden costs and interest. Let's break down the rent vs buy decision.
twitter_thread.tweet2: Banks sell you the dream of 'owning' an asset, but for the first decade, you mostly own a debt. Renting can actually build wealth faster if you invest the EMI difference.
twitter_thread.tweet3: The honest verdict: A home is a lifestyle choice, not always a financial one. If your EMI is double your rent, you are likely sacrificing your financial freedom for a brick-and-mortar trap.
twitter_thread.tweet4: Stop guessing and start calculating. Use our tool to see how you can save ₹2 Lakh over the next 5 years by choosing correctly. Full guide here: https://lastemi.com/blog/rent-vs-buy-decision

linkedin_post: The Indian middle class is often told that paying rent is equivalent to throwing money away. This narrative is heavily pushed by lenders who profit from your twenty-year commitment to a home loan. In reality, the rent vs buy decision is a complex financial calculation rather than a simple emotional milestone. When you account for property taxes, maintenance, and the opportunity cost of a down payment, renting often emerges as the smarter financial move in high-interest environments. Making the wrong choice could cost you over ₹2 Lakh in hidden expenses over just five years. At LastEMI, we believe in debt freedom over debt traps. A home loan remains a liability until the very last rupee is paid off. Before you sign mortgage papers, run the numbers to see if that EMI is actually serving your long-term wealth goals or just the bank's bottom line. Our latest guide breaks down the math so you can decide with clarity and honesty. https://lastemi.com/blog/rent-vs-buy-decision
whatsapp_message: Should you buy a home or keep renting? Most people think renting is a waste, but it could actually save you ₹2 Lakh over 5 years. Banks want you to take a loan, but we want you to be debt-free. Use our simple calculator to make the right rent vs buy decision for your future.
